behaviors.description: A cell containing `=SUM(A1:A10)` evaluates to the correct sum of the values in cells A1 through A10.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is SUM/range evaluation logic in one evaluator path; no required module handoff beyond local data access, no real OS/network/subprocess, and no AI output dependency. Rejection gate: no behavior text requires persistence, external infra, or multi-step orchestration.
behaviors.test_description: Call the formula evaluator on an in-memory cell range A1:A10 with fixed numeric fixtures and assert exact SUM result, including a couple of edge values (0, negative, decimal).

behaviors.description: A cell referencing another cell that contains a formula (e.g., `=B1+1` where B1 is `=A1*2`) produces the correct cascaded result.
behaviors.justification: Slot A: "cell referencing another cell that contains a formula ... produces the correct cascaded result." Slot B: handoff across modules (reference resolver + evaluator + dependency state). Slot C: if lowered to Unit, isolated function tests can miss incorrect propagation order/cached intermediate values between B1 and dependent cell. Integration-brake checks: no real OS/network/subprocess; no model-output dependency; no multi-agentic chain.
behaviors.test_description: Create an in-memory workbook with A1=3, B1=`=A1*2`, C1=`=B1+1`; run full recalculation through normal engine wiring and assert C1=7 and B1=6.

behaviors.description: The engine detects a circular dependency (A1 references B1, B1 references A1) and returns a circular reference error instead of looping.
behaviors.justification: Slot A: "detects a circular dependency ... returns a circular reference error instead of looping." Slot B: handoff between dependency graph construction and evaluator execution state. Slot C: if lowered to Unit, pure cycle-detector tests can miss runtime evaluator behavior that still loops or emits wrong error mapping. Rejection gate for System: no behavior text requires real subprocess/OS/network effects.
behaviors.test_description: Wire a workbook with A1=`=B1+1` and B1=`=A1+1`, invoke normal recalculation entrypoint, and assert a circular-reference error is surfaced and execution terminates promptly.

behaviors.description: VLOOKUP with an exact match returns the correct value from the lookup table, and returns an error when the key is not found.
behaviors.justification: This is built-in function semantics (exact-match lookup and not-found branch) and can fail entirely inside one function/object boundary with in-memory table values. Rejection gate: behavior text does not require persistence, OS/network/subprocess, or AI output.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke VLOOKUP evaluator with a small deterministic table fixture and assert matched return value and the expected not-found error code for a missing key.

behaviors.description: The IF function correctly handles nested conditions (e.g., `=IF(A1>10, IF(A1>20, "high", "medium"), "low")`).
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is expression evaluation/branching logic for nested IF; no required inter-module infra interaction beyond local expression execution. Rejection gate: behavior text is pure formula semantics.
behaviors.test_description: Evaluate the nested IF formula with A1 values spanning each branch (e.g., 5, 15, 25) and assert outputs `low`, `medium`, and `high`.

behaviors.description: CONCATENATE joins cell values with the correct separator and handles empty cells by skipping them rather than inserting blank strings.
behaviors.justification: This is deterministic string-join function behavior; real failures are in local CONCATENATE implementation (separator handling + empty-cell filtering), not infra boundaries. Rejection gate: no OS/network/subprocess or multi-module persistence requirement in behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Run CONCATENATE evaluation on fixtures with populated and empty cells plus explicit separator, asserting exact output string with empties skipped.

behaviors.description: A formula referencing a cell in a different sheet (e.g., `=Sheet2!A1+1`) resolves correctly when the referenced sheet exists.
behaviors.justification: Slot A: "referencing a cell in a different sheet ... resolves correctly." Slot B: handoff across workbook namespace/sheet resolver and evaluator modules. Slot C: if lowered to Unit, single-function eval tests can miss broken sheet lookup wiring (wrong sheet map/context passed). Rejection gate for System: behavior does not require real OS/network/subprocess.
behaviors.test_description: Build an in-memory workbook with Sheet2.A1=10 and Sheet1.A1=`=Sheet2!A1+1`; evaluate via normal engine API and assert Sheet1.A1=11.

behaviors.description: When a referenced cell's value changes, all dependent formulas are recalculated in the correct topological order.
behaviors.justification: Slot A: "all dependent formulas are recalculated in the correct topological order." Slot B: handoff/state propagation across dependency graph, invalidation, and recalculation scheduler modules. Slot C: if lowered to Unit, isolated sort/eval tests can miss stale cache and wrong propagation when modules interact after a cell update. Integration-brake: no real infra requirement, no AI dependency, no multi-agentic chain.
behaviors.test_description: Set a dependency chain/diamond in one workbook, mutate a root cell, run incremental recalc through public API, and assert both final values and observed recomputation order (or equivalent dependency-consistent state) match topology.

behaviors.description: The undo operation after editing a formula restores both the formula text and all previously computed dependent values.
behaviors.justification: Slot A: "undo ... restores both the formula text and all previously computed dependent values." Slot B: handoff between history/undo manager and recalculation engine state. Slot C: if lowered to Unit, testing undo stack alone can miss failure to restore dependent computed graph values after state rollback. Rejection gate for System: behavior text does not require OS/process/network effects.
behaviors.test_description: Edit a formula affecting dependents, verify new computed values, trigger undo via normal command path, then assert restored formula source and restored dependent outputs across impacted cells.

behaviors.description: Saving a workbook to disk and loading it back produces identical cell values and formulas — no data loss or precision change in floating-point results.
behaviors.justification: Slot A: "Saving a workbook to disk and loading it back ... no data loss or precision change." Slot B: module boundary between serializer/deserializer and workbook model (local filesystem allowed). Slot C: if lowered to Unit, encode/decode function tests can miss integration defects in full workbook persistence wiring (missed fields, formula metadata mapping). Rejection gate for System: behavior targets round-trip data fidelity, not OS-specific filesystem event/permission/network behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Round-trip a workbook with formulas and sensitive floating-point values through real save/load codepath (temp local file acceptable), then assert exact formula text equality and numeric equality at required precision for every populated cell.
